content.problemStatement:
Chef decided to watch a movie, and has just entered the theater.

The theater has 2N+12N+12N+1 seats arranged in a row. The seats are numbered 111 to 2N+12N+12N+1 from left to right.

Currently, the seats numbered 1,3,5,7,…,2N+11, 3, 5, 7, \ldots, 2N+11,3,5,7,…,2N+1 are occupied, and the seats numbered 2,4,6,…,2N2, 4, 6, \ldots, 2N2,4,6,…,2N are empty.

Chef must decide which empty seat to sit in.

For each i=1,2,3,…,N+1i = 1, 2, 3, \ldots, N+1i=1,2,3,…,N+1, The person sitting in seat 2i−12i-12i−1 has a hype level of AiA_iAi​.

That is, the person in seat 111 has a hype level of A1A_1A1​, the person in seat 333 has a hype level of A2A_2A2​, and so on.

Chef thinks the loudness of an empty seat is the maximum of the hype levels of the two people sitting next to him.

So, the loudness of seat 222 is the maximum of the hype levels of the people sitting in seats 111 and 333; the loudness of seat 444 is the maximum of the hype levels of the people sitting in seats 333 and 555; and so on.

To properly enjoy the movie, Chef would like to choose a seat whose loudness is as low as possible.

Can you help Chef by finding this minimum loudness?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — denoting that the number of seats in the theater is 2N+12N+12N+1.
The second line contains N+1N+1N+1 space separated integers A1,A2,…,AN+1A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{N+1}A1​,A2​,…,AN+1​, denoting the hype levels of the people in seats 1,3,5,…,2N+11, 3, 5, \ldots, 2N+11,3,5,…,2N+1, respectively.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single integer: the minimum possible loudness across all empty seats.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The hype values are [4,0,5][4, 0, 5][4,0,5] (where 000 denotes an unoccupied seat).
Chef can only choose to sit in seat 222, which has a loudness value of max⁡(4,5)=5\max(4, 5) = 5max(4,5)=5.
So, the answer is 555.
Test case 222: The hype values are [6,0,2,0,10][6, 0, 2, 0, 10][6,0,2,0,10] (where 000 denotes an unoccupied seat).
Chef can choose to sit in either seat 222 or seat 444.

Seat 222 has a loudness value of max⁡(6,2)=6\max(6, 2) = 6max(6,2)=6.
Seat 444 has a loudness value of max⁡(2,10)=10\max(2, 10) = 10max(2,10)=10.

666 is the smallest possible loudness.
